enrichment.summary: Kyle films a morning vlog from Boulder, describing a long work session the day before on a 3D map project called the NOMACCHI (a man-made lake in Rome). He also mentions helping his mom with household errands, listening to the History of Byzantium podcast, and abandoning a historical TV show in favor of fast-forwarding to visually useful scenes.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Abandoned it as just another stupid TV show
Only worth watching for wide-angle shots of castles and battlefields, not as entertainment
Tedious and time-consuming but glad to have it; excited about comparing it to Hayden Lake in Boulder

transcript.text: Good morning, living in Boulder vlog. Today is Sunday, so this vlog is for Saturday of the 11th show.
Ooh, I'm pretty high right now.
So yesterday I got up at like, I was working until like 6 and then I got up at like 11 or 12.
And I worked all day until 4 in the morning because, 3.30 or 4 in the morning because I had to get up today.
Um, it's 11.22 now. I had to get up to go and help my mom with stuff.
Uh, polish silver for like Christmas meal, help her buy something at the hardware store and clean the house and stuff.
So yesterday I worked on the NOMACCHI all day long. That's all I did all day long.
So, the NOMACCHI is the man-made lake in Rome, built by, I guess, just 500 meters across.
Um, and it's super complicated. Um, I'm not even, I mean, I could probably, I probably have to work all day again on it.
It's like two full days just on that because only like between a half and two thirds of it was actually,
I'm, I'm able to trace because it's that much, only like two thirds or half of it's on the map.
So, I have to, um, I have to, like an artist, draw out the rest of it.
Um, kind of like when I was building Boulder, which is like 10 times more time consuming than just tracing it.
So, I'm going to be happy when I'm done with the NOMACCHI, but I'm really glad that I have it.
I'm tripped out that it's the same size as Hayden Lake here in Boulder.
Pretty much the exact same size.
It's a pretty good idea to like put photos up on the internet of like, um,
The NOMACCHI and Hayden Lake soon proposed on top of each other as a picture.
And then not just that, but everything, every sister thing I have, have them superimposed,
Like the tribes to everything, buildings.
Um, yeah, so that's what I'll do today.
I'll go and work with my mom, come back, work on the NOMACCHI.
I probably won't even finish it today.
Or maybe I will.
I was listening to the History of Byzantium.
I'm not watching TV anymore.
I got about halfway through the first episode of Last Kingdom about,
Alfred the Great, and then I realized it's just another stupid TV show.
But, um, what I do want to do is go back and all those historical TV shows,
Just fast forward to the good parts, just so I know where they are.
And so I can see the, like the, their, uh, wide pans of the castles and battlefields and stuff like that.
So, uh, maybe even put it on screen captures on my website.
I did, I have a bunch of that collected on my other hard drive.
That I think died.
But, anyways, I think I've babbled enough.
Peace out.
